title: bud,run: runc does not support keep-groups
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

> /kind failing-test 

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Skip the final part of 2 tests that check `keep-groups` when the OCI runtime is `runc` as it doesn't support `keep-groups`.

#### How to verify it

https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/5101660

TAP files for the above job:
- https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/5101660/file/buildah-buildah-root.tap
- https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/5101660/file/buildah-buildah-user.tap
- Commands run: https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/5101660/logfile?filename=buildah-commands.txt

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

Currently the tests fail with:


comments.body: I don't understand why we're not noticing this test failure in this CI.  https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6406931759235072?logs=integration_test#L729 is one instance of it passing with runc.

> I don't understand why we're not noticing this test failure in this CI. https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6406931759235072?logs=integration_test#L729 is one instance of it passing with runc.

Is it running rootless and with supplemental groups?

Yes, it's on the list of things I'm working on. https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4642 is the upstream tracking issue for supporting this (as well as other crun extensions).

I was also working on a change to the runtime-spec's `features`, which would allow vendors to list what extensions they support (so that higher-level runtimes can know what is supported just by looking at `runc features`). This would also allow other runtimes to implement the same features. In this case, the `keep-groups` feature would be called something like `run.oci:keep-groups` (to copy the OpenZFS feature naming scheme -- the structure of the feature portion of the name would be up to the owner of the `run.oci` namespace in this case).

But we have been a little busy the past few months and I haven't had a chance to finish my spec proposal, nor the runc PR (even though it's a very simply feature to implement). I'll try to get it done as soon as I have some time.
(This would eventually allow you to decide whether to run a test like this based on `runtime features | jq '[.vendorExtensions[] | select(. == "run.oci:foobar")] | length > 0'.`)

> > I don't understand why we're not noticing this test failure in this CI. https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6406931759235072?logs=integration_test#L729 is one instance of it passing with runc.
> 
> Is it running rootless and with supplemental groups?

It wasn't, nice catch.  I'm trying to trigger the failure in #6227, but I must be missing something.
After a little prodding from @Luap99, I think #6228 triggers it, so LGTM.
